Title: The natural pyrazolotriazine pseudoiodinine from Pseudomonas mosselii 923 inhibits plant bacterial and fungal pathogens

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimicrobial metabolite/biopesticide active against Xanthomonas spp. and Magnaporthe oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for pseudoiodinine biosynthesis; deletion abolishes pseudoiodinine production and plant-protective antimicrobial activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; HPLC metabolite profiling; in planta biocontrol assay
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: proposed methylation step in pseudoiodinine assembly
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed as part of the psdABCDEFG operon; positively regulated by GacA-rsmY/rsmZ and negatively by CsrA proteins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psdA is part of the psdABCDEFG operon required for pseudoiodinine production. 2. Deletion of psdA abolished antibacterial activity and pseudoiodinine accumulation. 3. Complementation restored production and activity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LB versus TSB medium; genetic regulatory background involving GacA, rsmY/rsmZ and CsrA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target for operon overexpression and RBS engineering to boost pseudoiodinine yield in microbial biopesticide production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pseudoiodinine overproduction reduced early fermentation growth of P. mosselii
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: psdA promoter was used in transcriptional fusion assays and contains a 5' UTR/RBS region targeted by CsrA-mediated repression
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsrA1; CsrA2; CsrA3; rsmY; rsmZ; GacA indirectly

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for pseudoiodinine biosynthesis; deletion abolished production and antimicrobial activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; heterologous expression; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: glyoxylase/bleomycin resistance protein dioxygenase superfamily protein
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glyoxylase bleomycin resistance protein dioxygenase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psdB is one of seven genes defining the pseudoiodinine biosynthetic cluster. 2. Deletion eliminated pseudoiodinine and antibacterial activity. 3. Complementation or heterologous operon expression restored biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DSM17497 homolog orf2185 functionally complemented ΔpsdB
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: psdA; psdC; psdD; psdE; psdF; psdG

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for pseudoiodinine biosynthesis; deletion abolished production, but feeding 1,6-DDMT restored production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; chemical feeding with 1,6-DDMT; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: proposed to participate with/alongside PsdE in N-N bond formation and generation of 1,6-DDMT from ribitylaminouracil intermediate and glycine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psdC is essential for pseudoiodinine production. 2. Feeding 1,6-DDMT rescued pseudoiodinine production in the ΔpsdC mutant, placing psdC upstream of this intermediate. 3. psdC likely contributes to N-N bond formation in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: exact biochemical function remains unclear; DSM17497 homolog orf2186 complemented ΔpsdC

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for pseudoiodinine biosynthesis; initiates pathway from GTP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; sequence homology-based pathway assignment; heterologous operon expression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: proposed conversion of GTP into the first pyrimidinone intermediate at the start of pseudoiodinine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psdD encodes a GTP cyclohydrolase-like enzyme in the pseudoiodinine cluster. 2. Deletion abolished pseudoiodinine production and antimicrobial activity. 3. Pathway analysis indicates pseudoiodinine biosynthesis initiates from GTP via PsdD.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: assigned by homology to riboflavin/toxoflavin biosynthetic enzymes

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for pseudoiodinine biosynthesis; deletion abolished production, but feeding 1,6-DDMT restored production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; chemical feeding with 1,6-DDMT; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: proposed to participate with/alongside PsdC in N-N bond formation leading to 1,6-DDMT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psdE is essential for pseudoiodinine biosynthesis. 2. 1,6-DDMT feeding rescued the ΔpsdE mutant, placing psdE upstream of this intermediate. 3. psdE is implicated in the unclear step forming the fused N-N-containing scaffold.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for pseudoiodinine biosynthesis; deletion abolished production and antimicrobial activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; heterologous expression; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: proposed methylation step during conversion of late intermediate(s) to pseudoiodinine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psdF is part of the essential psdABCDEFG operon. 2. Deletion abolished pseudoiodinine production and antibacterial activity. 3. Complementation restored the phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contributes to pseudoiodinine biosynthesis; deletion partially attenuated production and antibacterial activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; heterologous expression; sequence homology-based pathway assignment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: proposed deamination/reduction step converting the initial GTP-derived intermediate toward the ribitylaminouracil branch leading to pseudoiodinine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psdG is a RibD-like gene in the pseudoiodinine cluster. 2. Deletion caused partial reduction rather than complete loss of pseudoiodinine and antibacterial activity. 3. Sequence similarity supports a role in early precursor conversion shared with riboflavin/toxoflavin-like chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: authors note psdG may affect structural or functional stability of pseudoiodinine; DSM17497 homolog orf2190 complemented ΔpsdG

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation (indirect of biosynthetic operon via sRNAs)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds and activates rsmY and rsmZ promoters; does not directly bind psdA promoter
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: uncharacterized signals perceived through GacS/GacA system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positive global regulator required for pseudoiodinine production; deletion abolishes metabolite accumulation and antibacterial activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn5 mutagenesis; markerless knockout; complementation; overexpression; EMSA; RNA-seq; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive upstream regulator required to activate the sRNA branch controlling pseudoiodinine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tn5 insertion and deletion of gacA abolished pseudoiodinine production and antagonistic activity. 2. GacA directly binds rsmY and rsmZ promoters but not the psdA promoter. 3. Complementation restored production, identifying GacA as a global positive regulator of the pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LB culture for transcriptome comparison; uncharacterized environmental inputs to GacS/GacA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: overexpression did not further increase pseudoiodinine production
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified as the single-copy Tn5 insertion mutant 34-6 defect site

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional derepression by sequestration of CsrA/RsmA proteins
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsrA1; CsrA2; CsrA3-bound psdA mRNA control
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates psd operon expression indirectly by antagonizing CsrA-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: activated by GacA binding to its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positive regulator; deletion significantly reduced pseudoiodinine production, and combined deletion with rsmZ nearly abolished production and antibacterial activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter motif analysis; EMSA for GacA binding; gene deletion; complementation; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: part of the key sRNA module linking GacA to relief of CsrA repression on pseudoiodinine synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rsmY promoter contains a GacA-binding motif and is directly bound by GacA. 2. rsmY positively regulates pseudoiodinine production. 3. Double deletion with rsmZ nearly abolishes metabolite production and antibacterial activity.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional derepression by sequestration of CsrA/RsmA proteins
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsrA1; CsrA2; CsrA3-bound psdA mRNA control
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates psd operon expression indirectly by antagonizing CsrA-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: activated by GacA binding to its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positive regulator; single deletion had little effect on production, but double deletion with rsmY abolished pseudoiodinine production and antibacterial activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter motif analysis; EMSA for GacA binding; gene deletion; complementation; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: functions redundantly with rsmY in the key GacA-dependent sRNA module controlling pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rsmZ promoter contains a GacA-binding motif and is directly bound by GacA. 2. rsmZ alone has limited effect but acts together with rsmY to maintain pseudoiodinine production. 3. Combined rsmY/rsmZ deletion nearly abolishes metabolite synthesis and antagonism.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional repression via binding GGA motif in 5' UTR/RBS
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: negative regulator of psdA promoter output, but single deletion did not significantly increase pseudoiodinine production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: deletion mutagenesis; psdA::uidA fusion assay; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: minor negative regulator relative to CsrA2/CsrA3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. csrA1 deletion increased psdA promoter-driven GUS activity. 2. Single csrA1 deletion did not significantly increase pseudoiodinine production. 3. csrA1 contributes to repression but is not the major determinant.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: deletion used in engineered overproducer strain

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional repression via binding GGA motif in 5' UTR/RBS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: strongly represses psdA expression and pseudoiodinine production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: major negative regulator; deletion significantly improves pseudoiodinine production and antibacterial activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: deletion mutagenesis; psdA::uidA fusion assay; HPLC metabolite profiling; engineered strain construction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major repressor whose removal is effective for pathway engineering to raise pseudoiodinine yield
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. csrA2 deletion increased psdA expression. 2. csrA2 loss significantly increased pseudoiodinine production. 3. csrA2 functions as a major negative regulator together with csrA3.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSB medium used during overproduction engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: removal contributed to higher yield but engineered overproducer showed slower early growth

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional repression via binding GGA motif in 5' UTR/RBS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: strongly represses psdA expression and pseudoiodinine production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: major negative regulator; deletion significantly improves pseudoiodinine production and antibacterial activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: deletion mutagenesis; psdA::uidA fusion assay; HPLC metabolite profiling; engineered strain construction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major repressor whose removal enables strong pathway derepression and overproduction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. csrA3 deletion increased psdA expression. 2. csrA3 loss significantly increased pseudoiodinine production. 3. csrA3 functions as a major negative regulator together with csrA2.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSB medium used during overproduction engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: removal contributed to higher yield but engineered overproducer showed slower early growth
